question_text: The cumulative probability we need to evaluate is: P(n < X ≤n + 3) = P(X = n + 1) + P(X = n + 2) + P(X = n + 3). Since pX(x) = �x−1  �3 x−1 �1 , we calculate the probability for each n and sum them up.

options.A: fter performing the necessary calculations and rounding off the result to two decimal places, we find that the value of the sum is approximately 2.31 . Quick Tip When working with geometric distributions, the probability mass function allows easy summation over ranges, as long as you understand the formula and apply it properly.
